2605.07990.title: Tool Calling is Linearly Readable and Steerable in Language Models
2605.07990.paper_summary: When a tool-calling agent picks the wrong tool, the failure is invisible until execution: the email gets sent, the meeting gets missed. Probing 12 instruction-tuned models across Gemma 3, Qwen 3, Qwen 2.5, and Llama 3.1 (270M to 27B), we find the identity of the chosen tool is linearly readable and steerable inside the model. Adding the mean-difference between two tools' average internal activations switches which tool the model selects at 77-100% accuracy on name-only single-turn prompts (93-100% at 4B+), and the JSON arguments that follow autoregressively match the new tool's schema, so flipping the name is enough. The same per-tool means also flag likely errors before they happen: on Gemma 3 12B and 27B, queries where the gap between the top-1 and top-2 tool is smallest produce 14-21x more wrong calls than queries with the largest gap. The causal effect concentrates along one direction, the row of the output layer that produces the target tool's first token: a unit vector along it at matched magnitude already reaches 93-100%, while what is left over leaves the choice almost untouched. Activation patching localises this to a small set of mid- and late-layer attention heads, and a within-topic probe across 14 same-domain $τ$-bench airline tools reaches top-1 61-89% across five 4B-14B models, ruling out the reading that we are just moving the model along a topic axis. Even base models encode the right tool before they can emit it: cosine readout from the internal state recovers 69-82% on BFCL while base generation reaches only 2-10%, suggesting pretraining forms the representation and instruction tuning later wires it to the output. We measure tool identity selection and JSON schema correctness in single-turn fixed-menu settings; multi-turn agentic transfer is more fragile and is discussed in Limitations.
2605.07990.paper_summary_zh: 當工具調用代理選擇錯誤的工具時，失敗在執行之前是不可見的：電子郵件被發送，會議被錯過。對 Gemma 3、Qwen 3、Qwen 2.5 和 Llama 3.1（270M 到 27B）中的 12 個指令調整模型進行探測，我們發現所選工具的身份在模型內是線性可讀和可操控的。添加兩個工具平均內部激活之間的均值差異可以在名稱僅單輪提示上以 77-100% 的準確率切換模型選擇的工具（在 4B+ 時為 93-100%），隨後自回歸的 JSON 參數與新工具的架構匹配，因此僅需翻轉名稱即可。每個工具的均值也能在錯誤發生之前標記出可能的錯誤：在 Gemma 3 的 12B 和 27B 上，當 top-1 和 top-2 工具之間的差距最小時，查詢產生的錯誤調用比差距最大的查詢多出 14-21 倍。因果效應集中在一個方向上，即產生目標工具第一個標記的輸出層行：沿著這個方向的單位向量在匹配的大小下已經達到 93-100%，而剩下的部分幾乎不影響選擇。激活修補將這一點局限於一小組中層和後層的注意力頭，並且在 14 個同域 $τ$-bench 航空工具之間進行的主題內探測在五個 4B-14B 模型中達到 top-1 61-89%，排除了我們僅僅在主題軸上移動模型的解讀。即使是基礎模型在能夠發出工具之前也編碼了正確的工具：從內部狀態的餘弦讀出在 BFCL 上恢復了 69-82%，而基礎生成僅達到 2-10%，這表明預訓練形成了表示，而指令調整則將其連接到輸出。我們在單輪固定菜單設置中測量工具身份選擇和 JSON 架構正確性；多輪代理轉移更脆弱，並在限制條件中進行討論。
2605.07990.author: Zekun Wu et.al.
2605.07990.authors: Zekun Wu, Ze Wang, Seonglae Cho, Yufei Yang, Adriano Koshiyama, Sahan Bulathwela, Maria Perez-Ortiz
